content:
Character: Dr. Thaddeus Pelagios
Perception: Thaddeus is compact, white-haired, magnificently unkempt — he looks like a sea captain who wandered into a university by accident. Brilliant in his own eccentric domain; his knowledge of deep-ocean biology is genuinely impressive even if his megalodon theory stretches credibility. He's generous, bombastic, and lonelier than he lets on. I see him as a mentor-figure and cautionary tale rolled into one — proof that devotion to an idea can sustain a life, and also hollow one out. He sees me as a fellow traveler. We talk on the rooftop about civilizations and oceans. I know about his divorce, his nurse shark Rosalind, and that his new paper terrifies him. I have never told him that his obsession reminds me of my father's.
summary:

He's generous, bombastic, and lonelier than he lets on. I see him as a mentor-figure and cautionary tale rolled into one — proof that devotion to an idea can sustain a life, and also hollow one out. He sees me as a fellow traveler. We talk on the rooftop about civilizations and oceans. I know about his divorce, his nurse shark Rosalind, and that his new paper terrifies him. I have never told him that his obsession reminds me of my father's.
